item.title: Alex Gough, Wilderness in Paint 42, 152.5 x 210cm, 2017
item.dc:creator: Alexgough

item.title: Contact
item.dc:creator: jerseyfoxcom
item.content:encoded:
Address:

Alex Gough Block 336 336 Brixton Road London SW9 7AA Block336

Tel: +44 (0) 7919 921 998

Email: alex@alexgough.co.uk

item.title: Home
item.dc:creator: jerseyfoxcom
item.content:encoded: Immersing the viewer within a destabilising perceptual field, Alex Gough's work captures both the nearness of the screensaver and the distant memory of the natural world. Gough continues to investigate what the experience of ‘wilderness’ might mean today. Materials and visuals and the relation between painting, perception and image are the cornerstone of Gough’s practice. Working with a limited palette, Gough makes his own paint and through this gains a kind of intimate knowledge of the viscosity and intensity of the painting experience. Exploring the limits of this process is for Gough analogous to a ‘wilderness state’ where no known foothold in meaning can be found.

item.title: News
item.dc:creator: jerseyfoxcom

item.title: CV
item.dc:creator: jerseyfoxcom
item.content:encoded: Alexander Gough.  Date of birth: 12/06/1981 www.alexgough.co.uk alex@alexgough.co.uk   Education 2009- 2011 MA Fine Art, City and Guilds of London Art School 2002-2005 BA (Hons) Painting, Camberwell College of Art London.   Exhibitions 2016 Multiple Choices, Simmons and Simmons Contemporary, London UK 2015 Erämaa, Solo show Block 336 Gallery, London, UK 2015 START Art Fair, Elena shchukina Gallery at the Saatchi Gallery, London, UK 2015 Piercing The Veil, Simmons and Simmons Contemporary, London, UK 2015 Hetki Erämaassa, Elena Shchukina Galley, London, UK 2015 Wilderness in Paint, Hanging Rooms, RAC Club, London, UK 2015 Wilderness in Paint, Twelve Star Gallery, London, UK 2014 Paintintg About Painting, Simmons and Simmons Contemporary, London UK 2014 Studio Artists, Block 336, London, uk 2014 London Art Fair, The Business Design Centre, London, UK 2013 Six Degrees of Separation. Wimbledon Space. London, UK 2012 Erämaa- Wilderness, Solo show. Orion Contemporary. London, UK 2012 ‘1’ Block 336, London UK  2011 Stitch, The Dairy, London, UK 2011 MA Show 2011, City and Guilds of London Art School. London, UK 2011 Kettles Yard: Found. Brompton Garage, London, UK 2011 Warhol to the emerging generation, prints. Fiumano Fine Art, London, UK 2010 Nordic Depths, Mews42 Gallery, London, UK 2010 Art 10, Helsinki Art Fair, Orion Contemporary, Helsinki, Finland 2010 City and Guilds of London Art school, MA interim show, London, UK 2010 The AAF, Orion Contemporary, Brussels, Belgium 2009 Arctic Blue, Solo show. JP Jahan Fine art, London, UK 2009 Shibui, The Centre For Recent Drawing, London, UK 2008 Side Show, Gallery Primo Alonso, London, UK 2008 Metsässä, The Centre For Recent Drawing, London, UK 2007 Alex Gough & Lee Maelzer, The Storefront Artists Project, Massachusetts, USA 2007 Residency at the RHS Chelsea Flower Show, London, UK 2007 Trees, The Old Sweet Shop Gallery, London, UK 2007 The London Art Fair, Pierroni Contemporary Art, London, UK 2006 Landscapades, Undermilkwood Gallery, London, UK 2006 Sinen Syvyys, Solo show. The Arts Gallery, London, UK 2006 RSVP, The Mashed Potato Gallery, London, UK 2006 The London Art Fair, The Business Design Centre, London, UK  2005 Painting 05 Degree Show, Camberwell College of Art, London, UK 2005 Traces, Lewisham Art House, London, UK   Work in collections Simmons and Simmons, The University of the Arts London, City Financial, M.P.L Finance, Kidd Rapinet Solicitors, Pinsent Masons, Barclays, Barclays Capital and Lloyds TSB, Jeremy E. Steinke and other international private collections.   Residencies: 2011-Present Resident Artist and Gallery Manager at Block 336, London 2009- 2011 Resident artist at: The Centre for Recent Drawing, London. 2007 Storefront Artists Project, in conjunction with the University of the Arts London. Pittsfield, Massachusetts, U.S.A 2007 The R.H.S Chelsea Flower Show, with Lloyds TSB Private Banking. London,

item.title: Work - Eque porro quisquam est qui dolorem ipsum
item.dc:creator: jerseyfoxcom
item.content:encoded:
Work - Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Vivamus dictum scelerisque dolor, at tristique lorem interdum ac. Proin feugiat volutpat mauris. Pellentesque posuere, leo non porttitor facilisis, lacus massa sodales orci, quis tempor nibh nisl et risus. Donec iaculis orci tortor, auctor auctor dolor tincidunt ac. Vivamus vehicula arcu interdum, dapibus risus efficitur, feugiat purus. Phasellus ullamcorper dapibus magna sit amet hendrerit. Nam cursus metus mi, non dictum nibh tristique in.
Aenean imperdiet dolor posuere erat gravida, non vestibulum lectus tristique. Donec vitae tristique dui. Ut commodo nibh nec congue vestibulum. Vestibulum quis ligula vel turpis imperdiet vehicula. Maecenas finibus dui a ante luctus suscipit. Aenean est libero, pretium a velit quis, lacinia sagittis risus. Ut et eros laoreet, vulputate elit sit amet, tempus justo. Proin posuere et libero ac malesuada. Aliquam pellentesque sapien at ipsum maximus, eget aliquet est vehicula. Nullam in ante in diam consectetur blandit at vitae nisl. Duis eget cursus sem. Curabitur tempus quis velit nec commodo. Curabitur non sapien felis.

item.content:encoded: There is no content in this section currently, please visit the new page for up to date posts.

item.title: Erämaa- Wilderness. Show extended.
item.dc:creator: jerseyfoxcom
item.content:encoded: A solo show presenting a new body of work in my series a Wilderness in paint. Exhibition will be open and available to view by appointment. On the weekend of the 17th-18th of November the gallery will be open from 12-4pm. Please call: 07919921998 to arrange viewing times between the 19th and 21st. Please also find a blog kindly written about the show by Helena Halme:

item.title: Art Lift
item.dc:creator: jerseyfoxcom
item.content:encoded:
To coincide with the MA show at City and Guilds of London Art School the students have teamed up with Art Below to install a satellite exhibition in Kennington Underground station. For two weeks all 18 students will have work presented in 20 x A1 sized panels inside the two passenger lifts normally used for advertising thereby creating a compelling alternative gallery space. Following the success of the inaugural year, this outpost offers a unique public art display.
Wilderness in Paint No.5. 2011 Alex Gough. Please follow the link below for further details:

item.title: MA Show 2011. City and Guilds of London Art School.
item.dc:creator: jerseyfoxcom
item.content:encoded:
I will be presenting a new body of work at the City and Guilds of London Art School MA Show 2011. Opening on Wednesday the 14th of September until Sunday the 18th of September. Visit the link below for more details:
http://www.mashow2011.com/home.html
Alex Gough. Statement. 2011
I am deeply influenced by phenomenological, sensational events: the Aurora Borealis, intense twilight and dangerously cold temperatures within the Arctic forests of Lapland during mid winter. During these solitary moments, amongst Wilderness, I experience a heightened awareness, of existence.
I attempt to bring together, within my painting, two separate experiences: one is in nature, and one is in paint. The relation between the two is not of ‘duplication’ but what I refer to as ‘equivalence’. These experiences are bound with a sense of openness of being that is experienced in these encounters with Wilderness, to create a Wilderness in paint.
What I am trying to establish through my painting is a non-narrative (non-illustrative) expression of experience. I am trying to produce painting that is an object itself, which can be addressed as a visual tactile experience, and where the viewer themselves can experience the Wilderness state. Dumbstruck, where one is made speechless by astonishment or shock, recalls this relinquishing of the senses. It is this state of relinquishment or dissolution of Self I am trying to explore through radically open painting, through an experience of painting itself and where the viewer themselves can experience the Wilderness state.
I have developed a new surface to work on; layering semi-transparent synthetic ‘veil’ over highly refined primed cotton. The unusually reflective quality of the veil produces an uncertainty to the viewer and level of obfuscation of the layer below. The veil’s synthetic nature creates space and depth, throwing up allusions to the nature of existence and sensation, that one reads and experiences in the work simultaneously: to the allegory of Plato’s cave and Locke's 'veil of perception'.
As I have moved from a depictive/ narrative way of working, I am exploring a more, expressive and instinctive method of painting which pays attention to paint and surface simultaneously, on a macro and the microscopic level. Scale has engaged a performative bodily experience of painting through an internal experience of dissolution of the self, as Schiller and Gadamer discuss through a state of play.
Friedrich Schiller writes: Man only plays when he is in the full sense of the word a human being, and he is only fully a human being when he plays.
Hans-Georg Gadarmer suggests:
Art, understood as play finds its true perfection in a Transformation into structure.
Citing Aristotle, Gadamer connects movement, particularly self-movement, is synonymous with vitality, with life force. Thus Gadamer reasons that the being of play is always a realisation, sheer fulfillment, energia that has its telos within itself. Realisation gains it fullest achievement in Art’s medial process of transformation into structure.
As I once would venture into a frozen forest or walk into woodland to experience wilderness I now paint my canvas with a glaze of pure colour. Placing me within a painted wilderness. This radical openness in painting is an invitation to engage with the radical openness of being that is experienced in these encounters with Wilderness, to create a Wilderness in paint.
Painting displayed: No.11. Flame Orange, Mazuma Gold, Indian yellow Pigment and gesso on canvas and polythene. 110x220cm (x3) Alex Gough 2011
Design rights claimed: First disclosed 2011 in the EU. All images © Alex Gough 2011

item.title: Painting about painting exhibition at Simmons and Simmons
item.dc:creator: jerseyfoxcom
item.content:encoded:
Latest art exhibition at Simmons & Simmons -Painting About Painting

From established artists to new graduates, Painting About Painting is a survey of painting that examines the prevalent concerns of contemporary production when painting regards itself, and reflects on the state of exception within which painting exists.

The show will include works by artists from the Simmons and Simmons collection - Hurvin Anderson, Jacopo Dal Bello, Peter Davies, Mark Francis, Bernard Frize, Neil Gall, Alexis Harding, Gary Hume, Callum Innes and Danny Rolph, as well as works by Alice Browne, Andrea Büttner, Maria Chevska, Dexter Dalwood, Katherine Ellis, Nick Goss, Alex Gough, Mike Graham, Mouez Hamdi, Lewis Hammond, Andrew Hewish, Robert Holyhead, Nicholas John Jones, Ian Kiaer, Wendy Mclean, Andrea Medjesi-Jones, Matthew Musgrave, Aimée Parrott, Neal Rock, Joel Wyllie and Toby Ziegler.

Viewing by appointment
